title: buddleja elegans subsp. angustata
intro: Buddleja elegans subsp. angustata, also known as the narrow-leaf butterfly bush, is a flowering plant species native to South America, characterized by its elongated, narrow leaves and vibrant purple flowers that attract pollinators. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Buddleja elegans subsp. angustata include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory congestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
